NKJV.1: “Take heed that you do not do your charitable deeds before men, to be seen by them. Otherwise you have no reward from your Father in heaven.
NKJV.2: Therefore,  when you do a charitable deed, do not sound a trumpet before you as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have glory from men. Assuredly, I say to you, they have their reward.
NKJV.3: But when you do a charitable deed, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
NKJV.4: that your charitable deed may be in secret; and your Father who sees in secret  will Himself reward you openly.
NKJV.5: “And when you pray, you shall not be like the hypocrites. For they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the corners of the streets, that they may be seen by men. Assuredly, I say to you, they have their reward.
NKJV.6: But you, when you pray, go into your room, and when you have shut your door, pray to your Father who is in the secret place; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you openly.
NKJV.7: And when you pray, do not use vain repetitions as the heathen do. For they think that they will be heard for their many words.
NKJV.8: “Therefore do not be like them. For your Father  knows the things you have need of before you ask Him.
NKJV.9: In this  manner, therefore, pray:  Our Father in heaven, Hallowed be Your  name.
NKJV.10: Your kingdom come. Your will be done On earth as it is in heaven.
NKJV.11: Give us this day our  daily bread.
NKJV.12: And  forgive us our debts, As we forgive our debtors.
NKJV.13: And do not lead us into temptation, But  deliver us from the evil one. For Yours is the kingdom and the power and the glory forever. Amen.
NKJV.14: “For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
NKJV.15: But  if you do not forgive men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
NKJV.16: “Moreover,  when you fast, do not be like the hypocrites, with a sad countenance. For they disfigure their faces that they may appear to men to be fasting. Assuredly, I say to you, they have their reward.
NKJV.17: But you, when you fast,  anoint your head and wash your face,
NKJV.18: so that you do not appear to men to be fasting, but to your Father who is in the secret place; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you openly.
NKJV.19: “Do not lay up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy and where thieves break in and steal;
NKJV.20: but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust destroys and where thieves do not break in and steal.
NKJV.21: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
NKJV.22: “The lamp of the body is the eye. If therefore your eye is good, your whole body will be full of light.
NKJV.23: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in you is darkness, how great is that darkness!
NKJV.24: “No one can serve two masters; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or else he will be loyal to the one and despise the other.  You cannot serve God and mammon.
NKJV.25: “Therefore I say to you,  do not worry about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink; nor about your body, what you will put on. Is not life more than food and the body more than clothing?
NKJV.26: Look at the birds of the air, for they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of more value than they?
NKJV.27: Which of you by worrying can add one cubit to his stature?
NKJV.28: “So why do you worry about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin;
NKJV.29: and yet I say to you that even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
NKJV.30: Now if God so clothes the grass of the field, which today is, and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will He not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?
NKJV.31: “Therefore do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’
NKJV.32: For after all these things the Gentiles seek. For your heavenly Father knows that you need all these things.
NKJV.33: But  seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things shall be added to you.
NKJV.34: Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about its own things. Sufficient for the day is its own trouble.

ESV.1: “Beware of practicing your righteousness before other people in order to be seen by them, for then you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
ESV.2: “Thus, when you give to the needy, sound no trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may be praised by others. Truly, I say to you, they have received their reward.
ESV.3: But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
ESV.4: so that your giving may be in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
ESV.5: “And when you pray, you must not be like the hypocrites. For they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and at the street corners, that they may be seen by others. Truly, I say to you, they have received their reward.
ESV.6: But when you pray, go into your room and shut the door and pray to your Father who is in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
ESV.7: “And when you pray, do not heap up empty phrases as the Gentiles do, for they think that they will be heard for their many words.
ESV.8: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
ESV.9: Pray then like this:  “Our Father in heaven,  hallowed be your name.
ESV.10: Your kingdom come,  your will be done,   on earth as it is in heaven.
ESV.11: Give us this day our daily bread,
ESV.12: and forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.
ESV.13: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.
ESV.14: For if you forgive others their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you,
ESV.15: but if you do not forgive others their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
ESV.16: “And when you fast, do not look gloomy like the hypocrites, for they disfigure their faces that their fasting may be seen by others. Truly, I say to you, they have received their reward.
ESV.17: But when you fast, anoint your head and wash your face,
ESV.18: that your fasting may not be seen by others but by your Father who is in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
ESV.19: “Do not lay up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust  destroy and where thieves break in and steal,
ESV.20: but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust destroys and where thieves do not break in and steal.
ESV.21: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
ESV.22: “The eye is the lamp of the body. So, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light,
ESV.23: but if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!
ESV.24: “No one can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and money.
ESV.25: “Therefore I tell you, do not be anxious about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink, nor about your body, what you will put on. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing?
ESV.26: Look at the birds of the air: they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of more value than they?
ESV.27: And which of you by being anxious can add a single hour to his span of life?
ESV.28: And why are you anxious about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin,
ESV.29: yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
ESV.30: But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which today is alive and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will he not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?
ESV.31: Therefore do not be anxious, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’
ESV.32: For the Gentiles seek after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them all.
ESV.33: But seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things will be added to you.
ESV.34: “Therefore do not be anxious about tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Sufficient for the day is its own trouble.

NASB.1: 'Beware of practicing your righteousness before men to be noticed by them; otherwise you have no reward with your Father who is in heaven.
NASB.2: 'So when you give to the poor, do not sound a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, so that they may be honored by men. Truly I say to you, they have their reward in full.
NASB.3: 'But when you give to the poor, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
NASB.4: so that your giving will be in secret; and your Father who sees [what is done] in secret will reward you.
NASB.5: 'When you pray, you are not to be like the hypocrites; for they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners so that they may be seen by men. Truly I say to you, they have their reward in full.
NASB.6: 'But you, when you pray, go into your inner room, close your door and pray to your Father who is in secret, and your Father who sees [what is done] in secret will reward you.
NASB.7: 'And when you are praying, do not use meaningless repetition as the Gentiles do, for they suppose that they will be heard for their many words.
NASB.8: 'So do not be like them; for your Father knows what you need before you ask Him.
NASB.9: 'Pray, then, in this way: 'Our Father who is in heaven, Hallowed be Your name.
NASB.10: 'Your kingdom come. Your will be done, On earth as it is in heaven.
NASB.11: 'Give us this day our daily bread.
NASB.12: 'And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.
NASB.13: 'And do not lead us into temptation, but deliver us from evil. [For Yours is the kingdom and the power and the glory forever. Amen].'
NASB.14: 'For if you forgive others for their transgressions, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
NASB.15: 'But if you do not forgive others, then your Father will not forgive your transgressions.
NASB.16: 'Whenever you fast, do not put on a gloomy face as the hypocrites [do], for they neglect their appearance so that they will be noticed by men when they are fasting. Truly I say to you, they have their reward in full.
NASB.17: 'But you, when you fast, anoint your head and wash your face
NASB.18: so that your fasting will not be noticed by men, but by your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees [what is done] in secret will reward you.
NASB.19: 'Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy, and where thieves break in and steal.
NASB.20: 'But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust destroys, and where thieves do not break in or steal;
NASB.21: for where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
NASB.22: 'The eye is the lamp of the body; so then if your eye is clear, your whole body will be full of light.
NASB.23: 'But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light that is in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!
NASB.24: 'No one can serve two masters; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
NASB.25: 'For this reason I say to you, do not be worried about your life, [as to] what you will eat or what you will drink; nor for your body, [as to] what you will put on. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing?
NASB.26: 'Look at the birds of the air, that they do not sow, nor reap nor gather into barns, and [yet] your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not worth much more than they?
NASB.27: 'And who of you by being worried can add a [single] hour to his life?
NASB.28: 'And why are you worried about clothing? Observe how the lilies of the field grow; they do not toil nor do they spin,
NASB.29: yet I say to you that not even Solomon in all his glory clothed himself like one of these.
NASB.30: 'But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which is [alive] today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, [will He] not much more [clothe] you? You of little faith!
NASB.31: 'Do not worry then, saying, 'What will we eat?' or 'What will we drink?' or 'What will we wear for clothing?'
NASB.32: 'For the Gentiles eagerly seek all these things; for your heavenly Father knows that you need all these things.
NASB.33: 'But seek first His kingdom and His righteousness, and all these things will be added to you.
NASB.34: 'So do not worry about tomorrow; for tomorrow will care for itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.

NLT.1: “Watch out! Don’t do your good deeds publicly, to be admired by others, for you will lose the reward from your Father in heaven.
NLT.2: When you give to someone in need, don’t do as the hypocrites do — blowing trumpets in the synagogues and streets to call attention to their acts of charity! I tell you the truth, they have received all the reward they will ever get.
NLT.3: But when you give to someone in need, don’t let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
NLT.4: Give your gifts in private, and your Father, who sees everything, will reward you.
NLT.5: “When you pray, don’t be like the hypocrites who love to pray publicly on street corners and in the synagogues where everyone can see them. I tell you the truth, that is all the reward they will ever get.
NLT.6: But when you pray, go away by yourself, shut the door behind you, and pray to your Father in private. Then your Father, who sees everything, will reward you.
NLT.7: “When you pray, don’t babble on and on as the Gentiles do. They think their prayers are answered merely by repeating their words again and again.
NLT.8: Don’t be like them, for your Father knows exactly what you need even before you ask him!
NLT.9:
Pray like this: Our Father in heaven,
may your name be kept holy.
NLT.10:
May your Kingdom come soon.
May your will be done on earth,
as it is in heaven.
NLT.11: Give us today the food we need,
NLT.12:
and forgive us our sins,
as we have forgiven those who sin against us.
NLT.13:
And don’t let us yield to temptation,
but rescue us from the evil one.
NLT.14: “If you forgive those who sin against you, your heavenly Father will forgive you.
NLT.15: But if you refuse to forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins.
NLT.16: “And when you fast, don’t make it obvious, as the hypocrites do, for they try to look miserable and disheveled so people will admire them for their fasting. I tell you the truth, that is the only reward they will ever get.
NLT.17: But when you fast, comb your hair and wash your face.
NLT.18: Then no one will notice that you are fasting, except your Father, who knows what you do in private. And your Father, who sees everything, will reward you.
NLT.19: “Don’t store up treasures here on earth, where moths eat them and rust destroys them, and where thieves break in and steal.
NLT.20: Store your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy, and thieves do not break in and steal.
NLT.21: Wherever your treasure is, there the desires of your heart will also be.
NLT.22: “Your eye is like a lamp that provides light for your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is filled with light.
NLT.23: But when your eye is unhealthy, your whole body is filled with darkness. And if the light you think you have is actually darkness, how deep that darkness is!
NLT.24: “No one can serve two masters. For you will hate one and love the other; you will be devoted to one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and be enslaved to money.
NLT.25: “That is why I tell you not to worry about everyday life — whether you have enough food and drink, or enough clothes to wear. Isn’t life more than food, and your body more than clothing?
NLT.26: Look at the birds. They don’t plant or harvest or store food in barns, for your heavenly Father feeds them. And aren’t you far more valuable to him than they are?
NLT.27: Can all your worries add a single moment to your life?
NLT.28: “And why worry about your clothing? Look at the lilies of the field and how they grow. They don’t work or make their clothing,
NLT.29: yet Solomon in all his glory was not dressed as beautifully as they are.
NLT.30: And if God cares so wonderfully for wildflowers that are here today and thrown into the fire tomorrow, he will certainly care for you. Why do you have so little faith?
NLT.31: “So don’t worry about these things, saying, ‘What will we eat? What will we drink? What will we wear?’
NLT.32: These things dominate the thoughts of unbelievers, but your heavenly Father already knows all your needs.
NLT.33: Seek the Kingdom of God above all else, and live righteously, and he will give you everything you need.
NLT.34: “So don’t worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will bring its own worries. Today’s trouble is enough for today.

WEB.1: “Be careful that you don’t do your charitable giving before men, to be seen by them, or else you have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
WEB.2: Therefore when you do merciful deeds, don’t sound a trumpet before yourself, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may get glory from men. Most certainly I tell you, they have received their reward.
WEB.3: But when you do merciful deeds, don’t let your left hand know what your right hand does,
WEB.4: so that your merciful deeds may be in secret, then your Father who sees in secret will reward you openly.
WEB.5: “When you pray, you shall not be as the hypocrites, for they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen by men. Most certainly, I tell you, they have received their reward.
WEB.6: But you, when you pray, enter into your inner room, and having shut your door, pray to your Father who is in secret, and your Father who sees in secret will reward you openly.
WEB.7: In praying, don’t use vain repetitions, as the Gentiles do; for they think that they will be heard for their much speaking.
WEB.8: Therefore don’t be like them, for your Father knows what things you need, before you ask him.
WEB.9: Pray like this: ‘Our Father in heaven, may your name be kept holy.
WEB.10: Let your Kingdom come. Let your will be done, as in heaven, so on earth.
WEB.11: Give us today our daily bread.
WEB.12: Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.
WEB.13: Bring us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. For yours is the Kingdom, the power, and the glory forever. Amen.’
WEB.14: “For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
WEB.15: But if you don’t forgive men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
WEB.16: “Moreover when you fast, don’t be like the hypocrites, with sad faces. For they disfigure their faces, that they may be seen by men to be fasting. Most certainly I tell you, they have received their reward.
WEB.17: But you, when you fast, anoint your head, and wash your face;
WEB.18: so that you are not seen by men to be fasting, but by your Father who is in secret, and your Father, who sees in secret, will reward you.
WEB.19: “Don’t lay up treasures for yourselves on the earth, where moth and rust consume, and where thieves break through and steal;
WEB.20: but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust consume, and where thieves don’t break through and steal;
WEB.21: for where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
WEB.22: “The lamp of the body is the eye. If therefore your eye is sound, your whole body will be full of light.
WEB.23: But if your eye is evil, your whole body will be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!
WEB.24: “No one can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other; or else he will be devoted to one and despise the other. You can’t serve both God and Mammon.
WEB.25: Therefore I tell you, don’t be anxious for your life: what you will eat, or what you will drink; nor yet for your body, what you will wear. Isn’t life more than food, and the body more than clothing?
WEB.26: See the birds of the sky, that they don’t sow, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns. Your heavenly Father feeds them. Aren’t you of much more value than they?
WEB.27: “Which of you, by being anxious, can add one moment  to his lifespan?
WEB.28: Why are you anxious about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow. They don’t toil, neither do they spin,
WEB.29: yet I tell you that even Solomon in all his glory was not dressed like one of these.
WEB.30: But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which today exists, and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, won’t he much more clothe you, you of little faith?
WEB.31: “Therefore don’t be anxious, saying, ‘What will we eat?’, ‘What will we drink?’ or, ‘With what will we be clothed?’
WEB.32: For the Gentiles seek after all these things; for your heavenly Father knows that you need all these things.
WEB.33: But seek first God’s Kingdom, and his righteousness; and all these things will be given to you as well.
WEB.34: Therefore don’t be anxious for tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Each day’s own evil is sufficient.

CSB17.1: “Be careful not to practice your righteousness <sup>[1]</sup> in front of others to be seen by them. Otherwise, you have no reward with your Father in heaven.
CSB17.2: So whenever you give to the poor, don’t sound a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be applauded by people. Truly I tell you, they have their reward. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.3: But when you give to the poor, don’t let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
CSB17.4: so that your giving may be in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you. <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.5: “Whenever you pray, you must not be like the hypocrites, because they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by people. Truly I tell you, they have their reward. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.6: But when you pray, go into your private room, shut your door, and pray to your Father who is in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you. <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.7: When you pray, don’t babble like the Gentiles, since they imagine they’ll be heard for their many words. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.8: Don’t be like them, because your Father knows the things you need before you ask him. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.9:
“Therefore, you should pray like this: ⓐ Our Father in heaven,
your name be honored as holy. ⓑ
CSB17.10:
Your kingdom come.
Your will be done
on earth as it is in heaven. ⓒ
CSB17.11: Give us today our daily bread. [4]
CSB17.12:
And forgive us our debts,
as we also have forgiven our debtors. ⓓ
CSB17.13:
And do not bring us into [5] temptation, ⓔ
but deliver us from the evil one. [6] ⓕ
CSB17.14: “For if you forgive others their offenses, your heavenly Father will forgive you as well. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.15: But if you don’t forgive others, <sup>[7]</sup> your Father will not forgive your offenses.
CSB17.16: “Whenever you fast, don’t be gloomy like the hypocrites. For they make their faces unattractive <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓗ</sup> so that their fasting is obvious to people. Truly I tell you, they have their reward.
CSB17.17: But when you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face, <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.18: so that your fasting isn’t obvious to others but to your Father who is in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you. <sup>[9]</sup>
CSB17.19: “Don’t store up for yourselves treasures <sup>[10]</sup> on earth, where moth and rust destroy and where thieves break in and steal. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.20: But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, <sup>ⓚ</sup> where neither moth nor rust destroys, and where thieves don’t break in and steal.
CSB17.21: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
CSB17.22: “The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
CSB17.23: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. So if the light within you is darkness, how deep is that darkness! <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.24: “No one can serve two masters, since either he will hate one and love the other, or he will be devoted to one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.  <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.25: “Therefore I tell you: <sup>ⓝ</sup> Don’t worry about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Isn’t life more than food and the body more than clothing? <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.26: Consider the birds of the sky: They don’t sow or reap or gather into barns, yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Aren’t you worth more than they? <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.27: Can any of you add one moment to his life-span <sup>[11]</sup> by worrying?
CSB17.28: And why do you worry about clothes? Observe how the wildflowers of the field grow: They don’t labor or spin thread.
CSB17.29: Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor was adorned like one of these.
CSB17.30: If that’s how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and thrown into the furnace tomorrow, won’t he do much more for you — you of little faith? <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.31: So don’t worry, saying, ‘What will we eat? ’ or ‘What will we drink? ’ or ‘What will we wear?’
CSB17.32: For the Gentiles eagerly seek all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
CSB17.33: But seek first the kingdom of God <sup>[12]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup> and his righteousness, <sup>ⓔ</sup> and all these things will be provided for you. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.34: Therefore don’t worry about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble <sup>ⓖ</sup> of its own.

AMP.1: "Be [very] careful not to do your good deeds publicly, to be seen by men; otherwise you will have no reward [prepared and awaiting you] with your Father who is in heaven.
AMP.2: "So whenever you give to the poor and do acts of kindness, do not blow a trumpet before you [to advertise it], as the hypocrites do [like actors acting out a role] in the synagogues and in the streets, so that they may be honored and recognized and praised by men. I assure you and most solemnly say to you, they [already] have their reward in full.
AMP.3: But when you give to the poor and do acts of kindness, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing [give in complete secrecy],
AMP.4: so that your charitable acts will be done in secret; and your Father who sees [what is done] in secret will reward you.
AMP.5: "Also, when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites; for they love to pray [publicly] standing in the synagogues and on the corners of the streets so that they may be seen by men. I assure you and most solemnly say to you, they [already] have their reward in full.
AMP.6: But when you pray, go into your most private room, close the door and pray to your Father who is in secret, and your Father who sees [what is done] in secret will reward you.
AMP.7: "And when you pray, do not use meaningless repetition as the Gentiles do, for they think they will be heard because of their many words.
AMP.8: So do not be like them [praying as they do]; for your Father knows what you need before you ask Him.
AMP.9:
"Pray, then, in this way:
'Our Father who is in heaven,
Hallowed be Your name.
AMP.10:
' Your kingdom come,
Your will be done
On earth as it is in heaven.
AMP.11: 'Give us this day our daily bread.
AMP.12: 'And forgive us our debts, as we have forgiven our debtors [letting go of both the wrong and the resentment].
AMP.13: 'And do not lead us into temptation, but deliver us from evil.[For Yours is the kingdom and the power and the glory forever. Amen.]'
AMP.14: For if you forgive others their trespasses [their reckless and willful sins], your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
AMP.15: But if you do not forgive others [nurturing your hurt and anger with the result that it interferes with your relationship with God], then your Father will not forgive your trespasses.
AMP.16: "And whenever you are fasting, do not look gloomy like the hypocrites, for they put on a sad and dismal face [like actors, discoloring their faces with ashes or dirt] so that their fasting may be seen by men. I assure you and most solemnly say to you, they [already] have their reward in full.
AMP.17: But when you fast, put oil on your head [as you normally would to groom your hair] and wash your face
AMP.18: so that your fasting will not be noticed by people, but by your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees [what is done] in secret will reward you.
AMP.19: "Do not store up for yourselves [material] treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy, and where thieves break in and steal.
AMP.20: But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust destroys, and where thieves do not break in and steal;
AMP.21: for where your treasure is, there your heart [your wishes, your desires; that on which your life centers] will be also.
AMP.22: "The eye is the lamp of the body; so if your eye is clear [spiritually perceptive], your whole body will be full of light [benefiting from God's precepts].
AMP.23: But if your eye is bad [spiritually blind], your whole body will be full of darkness [devoid of God's precepts]. So if the [very] light inside you [your inner self, your heart, your conscience] is darkness, how great and terrible is that darkness!
AMP.24: "No one can serve two masters; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and mammon [money, possessions, fame, status, or whatever is valued more than the Lord].
AMP.25: "Therefore I tell you, stop being worried or anxious (perpetually uneasy, distracted) about your life, as to what you will eat or what you will drink; nor about your body, as to what you will wear. Is life not more than food, and the body more than clothing?
AMP.26: Look at the birds of the air; they neither sow [seed] nor reap [the harvest] nor gather [the crops] into barns, and yet your heavenly Father keeps feeding them. Are you not worth much more than they?
AMP.27: And who of you by worrying can add one hour to [the length of] his life?
AMP.28: And why are you worried about clothes? See how the lilies and wildflowers of the field grow; they do not labor nor do they spin [wool to make clothing],
AMP.29: yet I say to you that not even Solomon in all his glory and splendor dressed himself like one of these.
AMP.30: But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which is alive and green today and tomorrow is [cut and] thrown [as fuel] into the furnace, will He not much more clothe you? You of little faith!
AMP.31: Therefore do not worry or be anxious (perpetually uneasy, distracted), saying, 'What are we going to eat?' or 'What are we going to drink?' or 'What are we going to wear?'
AMP.32: For the [pagan] Gentiles eagerly seek all these things; [but do not worry,] for your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
AMP.33: But first and most importantly seek (aim at, strive after) His kingdom and His righteousness [His way of doing and being right—the attitude and character of God], and all these things will be given to you also.
AMP.34: "So do not worry about tomorrow; for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.

MSG.1: "Be especially careful when you are trying to be good so that you don't make a performance out of it. It might be good theater, but the God who made you won't be applauding.
MSG.2: "When you do something for someone else, don't call attention to yourself. You've seen them in action, I'm sure—'playactors' I call them—treating prayer meeting and street corner alike as a stage, acting compassionate as long as someone is watching, playing to the crowds. They get applause, true, but that's all they get.
MSG.3: When you help someone out, don't think about how it looks.
MSG.4: Just do it—quietly and unobtrusively. That is the way your God, who conceived you in love, working behind the scenes, helps you out.
MSG.5: "And when you come before God, don't turn that into a theatrical production either. All these people making a regular show out of their prayers, hoping for stardom! Do you think God sits in a box seat?
MSG.6: "Here's what I want you to do: Find a quiet, secluded place so you won't be tempted to role-play before God. Just be there as simply and honestly as you can manage. The focus will shift from you to God, and you will begin to sense his grace.
MSG.7: "The world is full of so-called prayer warriors who are prayer-ignorant. They're full of formulas and programs and advice, peddling techniques for getting what you want from God.
MSG.8: Don't fall for that nonsense. This is your Father you are dealing with, and he knows better than you what you need.
MSG.9: With a God like this loving you, you can pray very simply. Like this: Our Father in heaven, Reveal who you are.
MSG.10: Set the world right; Do what's best— as above, so below.
MSG.11: Keep us alive with three square meals.
MSG.12: Keep us forgiven with you and forgiving others.
MSG.13: Keep us safe from ourselves and the Devil. You're in charge! You can do anything you want! You're ablaze in beauty! Yes. Yes. Yes.
MSG.14: "In prayer there is a connection between what God does and what you do. You can't get forgiveness from God, for instance, without also forgiving others.
MSG.15: If you refuse to do your part, you cut yourself off from God's part.
MSG.16: "When you practice some appetite-denying discipline to better concentrate on God, don't make a production out of it. It might turn you into a small-time celebrity but it won't make you a saint.
MSG.17: If you 'go into training' inwardly, act normal outwardly. Shampoo and comb your hair, brush your teeth, wash your face.
MSG.18: God doesn't require attention-getting devices. He won't overlook what you are doing; he'll reward you well.
MSG.19: "Don't hoard treasure down here where it gets eaten by moths and corroded by rust or—worse!—stolen by burglars.
MSG.20: Stockpile treasure in heaven, where it's safe from moth and rust and burglars.
MSG.21: It's obvious, isn't it? The place where your treasure is, is the place you will most want to be, and end up being.
MSG.22: "Your eyes are windows into your body. If you open your eyes wide in wonder and belief, your body fills up with light.
MSG.23: If you live squinty-eyed in greed and distrust, your body is a dank cellar. If you pull the blinds on your windows, what a dark life you will have!
MSG.24: "You can't worship two gods at once. Loving one god, you'll end up hating the other. Adoration of one feeds contempt for the other. You can't worship God and Money both.
MSG.25: "If you decide for God, living a life of God-worship, it follows that you don't fuss about what's on the table at mealtimes or whether the clothes in your closet are in fashion. There is far more to your life than the food you put in your stomach, more to your outer appearance than the clothes you hang on your body.
MSG.26: Look at the birds, free and unfettered, not tied down to a job description, careless in the care of God. And you count far more to him than birds.
MSG.27: "Has anyone by fussing in front of the mirror ever gotten taller by so much as an inch?
MSG.28: All this time and money wasted on fashion—do you think it makes that much difference? Instead of looking at the fashions, walk out into the fields and look at the wildflowers. They never primp or shop,
MSG.29: but have you ever seen color and design quite like it? The ten best-dressed men and women in the country look shabby alongside them.
MSG.30: "If God gives such attention to the appearance of wildflowers—most of which are never even seen—don't you think he'll attend to you, take pride in you, do his best for you?
MSG.31: What I'm trying to do here is to get you to relax, to not be so preoccupied with getting, so you can respond to God's giving.
MSG.32: People who don't know God and the way he works fuss over these things, but you know both God and how he works.
MSG.33: Steep your life in God-reality, God-initiative, God-provisions. Don't worry about missing out. You'll find all your everyday human concerns will be met.
MSG.34: "Give your entire attention to what God is doing right now, and don't get worked up about what may or may not happen tomorrow. God will help you deal with whatever hard things come up when the time comes.

NIV.1:
Giving to the Needy
“Be careful not to do your ‘acts of righteousness’ before men, to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven.
NIV.2: “So when you give to the needy, do not announce it with trumpets, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honored by men. I tell you the truth, they have received their reward in full.
NIV.3: But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
NIV.4: so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
NIV.5:
Prayer
“And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by men. I tell you the truth, they have received their reward in full.
NIV.6: But when you pray, go into your room, close the door and pray to your Father, who is unseen. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
NIV.7: And when you pray, do not keep on babbling like pagans, for they think they will be heard because of their many words.
NIV.8: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
NIV.9:
“This, then, is how you should pray:
“‘Our Father in heaven,
hallowed be your name,
NIV.10:
your kingdom come,
your will be done
on earth as it is in heaven.
NIV.11: Give us today our daily bread.
NIV.12:
Forgive us our debts,
as we also have forgiven our debtors.
NIV.13:
And lead us not into temptation,
but deliver us from the evil one.’
NIV.14: For if you forgive men when they sin against you, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
NIV.15: But if you do not forgive men their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins.
NIV.16:
Fasting
“When you fast, do not look somber as the hypocrites do, for they disfigure their faces to show men they are fasting. I tell you the truth, they have received their reward in full.
NIV.17: But when you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face,
NIV.18: so that it will not be obvious to men that you are fasting, but only to your Father, who is unseen; and your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
NIV.19:
Treasures in Heaven
“Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy, and where thieves break in and steal.
NIV.20: But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where moth and rust do not destroy, and where thieves do not break in and steal.
NIV.21: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
NIV.22: “The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are good, your whole body will be full of light.
NIV.23: But if your eyes are bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great is that darkness!
NIV.24: “No one can serve two masters. Either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and Money.
NIV.25:
Do Not Worry
“Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more important than food, and the body more important than clothes?
NIV.26: Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they?
NIV.27: Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?
NIV.28: “And why do you worry about clothes? See how the lilies of the field grow. They do not labor or spin.
NIV.29: Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of these.
NIV.30: If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, will he not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?
NIV.31: So do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’
NIV.32: For the pagans run after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
NIV.33: But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
NIV.34: Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.

NRSVCE.1: “Beware of practicing your piety before others in order to be seen by them; for then you have no reward from your Father in heaven.
NRSVCE.2: “So whenever you give alms, do not sound a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, so that they may be praised by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward.
NRSVCE.3: But when you give alms, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
NRSVCE.4: so that your alms may be done in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
NRSVCE.5: “And whenever you pray, do not be like the hypocrites; for they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and at the street corners, so that they may be seen by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward.
NRSVCE.6: But whenever you pray, go into your room and shut the door and pray to your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
NRSVCE.7: “When you are praying, do not heap up empty phrases as the Gentiles do; for they think that they will be heard because of their many words.
NRSVCE.8: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
NRSVCE.9:
“Pray then in this way: Our Father in heaven,
hallowed be your name.
NRSVCE.10:
Your kingdom come.
Your will be done,
on earth as it is in heaven.
NRSVCE.11: Give us this day our daily bread.
NRSVCE.12:
And forgive us our debts,
as we also have forgiven our debtors.
NRSVCE.13:
And do not bring us to the time of trial,
but rescue us from the evil one.
NRSVCE.14: For if you forgive others their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you;
NRSVCE.15: but if you do not forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
NRSVCE.16: “And whenever you fast, do not look dismal, like the hypocrites, for they disfigure their faces so as to show others that they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward.
NRSVCE.17: But when you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face,
NRSVCE.18: so that your fasting may be seen not by others but by your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
NRSVCE.19: “Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust consume and where thieves break in and steal;
NRSVCE.20: but store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust consumes and where thieves do not break in and steal.
NRSVCE.21: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
NRSVCE.22: “The eye is the lamp of the body. So, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light;
NRSVCE.23: but if your eye is unhealthy, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!
NRSVCE.24: “No one can serve two masters; for a slave will either hate the one and love the other, or be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
NRSVCE.25: “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink, or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing?
NRSVCE.26: Look at the birds of the air; they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of more value than they?
NRSVCE.27: And can any of you by worrying add a single hour to your span of life?
NRSVCE.28: And why do you worry about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they neither toil nor spin,
NRSVCE.29: yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not clothed like one of these.
NRSVCE.30: But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which is alive today and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will he not much more clothe you — you of little faith?
NRSVCE.31: Therefore do not worry, saying, ‘What will we eat?’ or ‘What will we drink?’ or ‘What will we wear?’
NRSVCE.32: For it is the Gentiles who strive for all these things; and indeed your heavenly Father knows that you need all these things.
NRSVCE.33: But strive first for the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
NRSVCE.34: “So do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will bring worries of its own. Today’s trouble is enough for today.

NET.1: “Be careful not to display your righteousness merely to be seen by people. Otherwise you have no reward with your Father in heaven.
NET.2: Thus whenever you do charitable giving, do not blow a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in synagogues and on streets so that people will praise them. I tell you the truth, they have their reward.
NET.3: But when you do your giving, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
NET.4: so that your gift may be in secret. And your Father, who sees in secret, will reward you.
NET.5: “Whenever you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, because they love to pray while standing in synagogues and on street corners so that people can see them. Truly I say to you, they have their reward.
NET.6: But whenever you pray, go into your room, close the door, and pray to your Father in secret. And your Father, who sees in secret, will reward you.
NET.7: When you pray, do not babble repetitiously like the Gentiles, because they think that by their many words they will be heard.
NET.8: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
NET.9: So pray this way: Our Father in heaven, may your name be honored,
NET.10: may your kingdom come, may your will be done on earth as it is in heaven.
NET.11: Give us today our daily bread,
NET.12: and forgive us our debts, as we ourselves have forgiven our debtors.
NET.13: And do not lead us into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one.
NET.14: “For if you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
NET.15: But if you do not forgive others, your Father will not forgive you your sins.
NET.16: “When you fast, do not look sullen like the hypocrites, for they make their faces unattractive so that people will see them fasting. I tell you the truth, they have their reward.
NET.17: When you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face,
NET.18: so that it will not be obvious to others when you are fasting, but only to your Father who is in secret. And your Father, who sees in secret, will reward you.
NET.19: “Do not accumulate for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy and where thieves break in and steal.
NET.20: But accumulate for yourselves treasures in heaven, where moth and rust do not destroy, and thieves do not break in and steal.
NET.21: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
NET.22: “The eye is the lamp of the body. If then your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
NET.23: But if your eye is diseased, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!
NET.24: “No one can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and money.
NET.25: “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink, or about your body, what you will wear. Isn’t there more to life than food and more to the body than clothing?
NET.26: Look at the birds in the sky: They do not sow, or reap, or gather into barns, yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Aren’t you more valuable than they are?
NET.27: And which of you by worrying can add even one hour to his life?
NET.28: Why do you worry about clothing? Think about how the flowers of the field grow; they do not work or spin.
NET.29: Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his glory was clothed like one of these!
NET.30: And if this is how God clothes the wild grass, which is here today and tomorrow is tossed into the fire to heat the oven, won’t he clothe you even more, you people of little faith?
NET.31: So then, don’t worry saying, ‘What will we eat?’ or ‘What will we drink?’ or ‘What will we wear?’
NET.32: For the unconverted pursue these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
NET.33: But above all pursue his kingdom and righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
NET.34: So then, do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Today has enough trouble of its own.
